technology.description: Azure Stack HCI Review is a Preview family with 52 normalized items, 13 high-severity findings, covers Backup and Disaster Recovery, Management and Monitoring, Networking, and uses generalized guidance.
technology.whatThisMeans: Use to enrich design reviews, but validate recommendations before treating them as baseline controls.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: All planned storage pools should use direct-attached storage (SATA, SAS, NVMe)
Disks are symmetrical across all nodes
Parity type disk redundancy should only be used for low I/O volumes (backup/archive)
Ensure there at least 2 capacity disks with available capacity in the Storage Pool
'Delimited allocation' has been considered to improve volume resiliency in a multi-node failure
CSVs are created in multiples of node count
If a cache tier is implemented, the number of capacity drives is a multiple of the number of cache drives
A minimum of 1 type of each disk type per node has been factored as a reserve disk
VMFleet has been run prior to workload deployment to baseline storage performance
items.description: VMFleet is a tool that can be used to measure the performance of a storage subsystem, best used to baseline performance prior to workload deployment

items.text: OS drives use a dedicated storage controller
CSV in-memory read caching is enabled and properly configured
NICs are symmetrical across nodes
Storage networking is redundant
Host networking configuration is managed by Network ATC and intents are healthy
Network HUD has been configured
Storage NICs are assigned static IP addresses on separate subnets and VLANs
For switchless designs, dual link full mesh connectivity has been implemented
If the cluster is made up of more than 3 nodes, a switched storage network has been implemented
RDMA is enabled on the Storage networking
Test-RDMA.ps1 has been run to validate the RDMA configuration
If a VMSwitch is shared for Compute and Management traffic, require that Management traffic is tagged with a VLAN ID
items.description: This ensures that Management traffic is not exposed to the VM traffic

items.text: There are at least 3 Network Controller VMs deployed
items.description: This ensures you have at least 3 NCs active at all times during NC upgrades.

items.text: Backups of SDN infrastructure are configured and tested
SCOM Managed Instance has been considered for more complex monitoring and alerting scenarios
Alerts have been configured for the cluster, either using Azure Monitor, SCOM, or a third-party solution
Insights has been enabled at the cluster level and all nodes are reporting data
Azure Monitoring Agent has been deployed to hosts and an appropriate Data Collection Rule has been configured
Relevant hardware monitoring has been configured
Relevant hardware alerting has been configured
The Azure CLI has been installed on every node to enable RB management from WAC
DHCP is available in the cluster to support Guest Configuration at VM deployment from Azure
Backups of HCI VMs have been configured using MABS or a third-party solution
Cluster configuration or a configuration script has been documented and maintained
A cluster witness has been configured for clusters with less than 5 nodes
Cluster-Aware Updating has been configured for Windows and hardware updates (if available)
Cluster validation has been run against the configured cluster
Azure Benefits has been enabled at the cluster and VM levels
The Environment Checker module has been run to validate the environment

Group Policy inheritance on the HCI cluster and node Active Directory organizational unit has been blocked or applied policies have been evaluated for compatibility issues (usually WinRM and PowerShell execution policy)
WAC is on the latest release and configured to automatically upgrade extensions
There is sub 5ms latency between each site if synchronous replication is being configured AAD
Management, Replication and Storage networks excluded from stretched VLANs configurations, are routed, and in different subnets
There is a plan detailed for site failure and recovery
Separate vLANs and networks are used for each replication network across both sites
Use either a cloud witness or a file share witness in a third site for cluster quorum for clusters with less than 5 nodes
When using data deduplication, only enable it on the primary/source volumes
Storage backing log volumes must be faster (ideally) or at least as fast as capacity storage
Azure Site Recovery has been considered for DR purposes
BitLocker has been enabled on CSVs for volume encryption, where appropriate
SMB encryption has been enabled, where appropriate
Microsoft Defender Antivirus has been enabled on all nodes
Credential Guard has been configured, where appropriate
